channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: A long-awaited Gaza ceasefire starts after a delay
item.description: A ceasefire in the Gaza Strip took effect on Sunday following an almost three-hour delay after Hamas was late to name the three hostage it plans to release. The announcement from Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office said the ceasefire began at 11:15 a.m. local time (0915 GMT).
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Warm weather threatens Epiphany ice water plunges for Russia's Orthodox Christians
item.description: Thousands of Russians celebrating the Orthodox Christian feast day of Epiphany, where worshippers bathe in the icy waters of frozen lakes and rivers, have been forced to cancel their traditional ceremonies due to unusually warm winter temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taliban deputy tells leader there is no excuse for education bans on Afghan women and girls
item.description: A senior Taliban figure has urged the group's leader to scrap education bans on Afghan women and girls, saying there is no excuse for them, in a rare public rebuke of government policy.
item.dc:creator: THE ASSOCIATED PRESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US rejects Venezuelan President Maduro's reelection, but keeps financial lifeline for his government
item.description: In 2022, the White House granted Venezuela a financial lifeline “to support the restoration of democracy” after President Nicolás Maduro promised to work toward an open presidential election, granting U.S. energy giant Chevron a permit to pump and export Venezuelan oil despite sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Regina Garcia Cano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An imprisoned Nobel laureate underscores human rights abuses in Belarus
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — The yellow name tag that Ales Bialiatski wears on his prison garb sets him apart from other inmates in Penal Colony No. 9 in eastern Belarus. It marks Bialiatski as a political prisoner to be singled out for harsh treatment.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — After more than a year of devastating war, the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is set to begin on Sunday, pausing the 15-month conflict between Israel and the Palestinian militant group in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Journalists in Haiti demand justice as they bury a second colleague killed by gangs
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — The young boy wore a suit and bow tie to his stepfather’s funeral on Saturday, where he demanded a handkerchief and wiped tears off his mother’s face as they buried Marckendy Natoux, one of two journalists killed by gangs in Haiti.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Stray dog brings sick, freezing puppy to vet's office
item.description: A stray dog was caught on camera carrying her sick puppy to the front door of a veterinarian clinic in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump inherits fragile Gaza ceasefire
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas, expected to begin its first phase Sunday, has brought relief to the international community but questions about whether the truce will hold — an issue that President-elect Trump will have to oversee when he takes office next week.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: More than 40 Pakistanis feared drowned in the capsizing of a migrant boat off African coast
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — More than 40 Pakistanis were feared to have drowned in the capsizing of a boat off West Africa's Atlantic coastline, which has emerged as a primary point of departure for migrants aiming to reach Europe.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's impeached president is arrested over a martial law declaration as his supporters riot
item.description: South Korea’s impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, has been formally arrested, days after being apprehended at his presidential compound in Seoul.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel's full Cabinet approves the Gaza ceasefire and hostage release deal
item.description: Israel's Cabinet has approved the Gaza ceasefire deal that would pause the fighting and release dozens of hostages held by militants, along with Palestinians imprisoned by Israel. The approval came early on Saturday after a marathon session by the full Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ancient Pompeii excavation uncovers lavish private bath complex
item.description: A private bath complex in Pompeii has been unearthed by archaeologists; the baths highlight the wealth of the ancient city before it was destroyed in AD 79.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blessings for dogs? Bring them to Mexico City's cathedral and St. Anthony will do the rest
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Behaving at their best, a dozen dogs attended Mass at Mexico City’s cathedral Friday, waiting for their turn to be doused with holy water. The blessing of the animals is a long-time Catholic tradition celebrated on January 17.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli security experts argue ‘bad' deal with Hamas, but ‘no other way’ to free hostages
item.description: Security experts analyzing the deal brokered between Israel and Hamas argue it was a 'bad deal' as it could pose security risks for Israel but one that needed to be done to return the hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 lawyers for the late Kremlin foe Alexei Navalny are convicted and sentenced to prison
item.description: PETUSHKI, Russia (AP) — Three lawyers who once represented the late Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny were convicted by a court Friday as part of the Kremlin’s crackdown on dissent that has reached levels unseen since Soviet times.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump and Chinese leader Xi talk about trade, fentanyl and TikTok
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping discussed trade, fentanyl and TikTok in a phone call Friday, just days before Trump heads back to the White House with vows to impose tariffs and other measures on America's biggest rival.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: International Criminal Court's chief prosecutor meets with Syrian leader in Damascus
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor Karim Khan made an unannounced visit Friday to Damascus to confer with the leader of Syria’s de facto government on how to ensure accountability for alleged crimes committed in the country.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Xi discuss TikTok, trade in call before inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump said Friday he spoke with Chinese President Xi Jinping, with the two leaders discussing trade and the fate of the popular video-sharing app TikTok. "The call was a very good one for both China and the U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: French president in Lebanon to meet the country's new leaders and discuss ceasefire with Israel
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — France’s president began a visit to Lebanon Friday, where he will meet the crisis-hit country’s newly elected leaders, as the nation attempts to recover from the 14-month Israel-Hezbollah war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel, Hamas ceasefire poised to begin Sunday
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is poised to begin Sunday, after the Israeli security Cabinet on Friday endorsed the deal covering it and the release of hostages and called for the full government to agree to the terms.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Xi's special representative Han Zheng to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: China’s vice president will attend President-elect Trump’s inauguration, according to a Thursday statement from the Foreign Ministry Spokesperson. “At U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Canada sends Trump 'message' with retaliatory tariff talk
item.description: Canada must send a “message” to President-elect Trump on tariffs, one of the country's provincial leaders said Wednesday alongside Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, who described the threatened levies as unnecessary.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Brazilian court denies Bolsonaro’s request to travel to Trump’s inauguration
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court has denied former President Jair Bolsonaro's request to have his passport temporarily restored in order to attend the inauguration of U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK leader Starmer signs ‘100-year partnership’ agreement with Ukraine during trip to Kyiv
item.description: As part of a European show of support, U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer has signed a 100-year partnership agreement with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis injured as Vatican confirms 2nd fall in matter of weeks
item.description: Pope Francis fell for the second time in six weeks on Thursday, injuring his right arm and requiring a sling, the Vatican says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis hurts his right arm after falling for the second time in just over a month
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis fell Thursday and hurt his right arm, the Vatican said, just weeks after another apparent fall resulted in a bad bruise on his chin. Francis didn’t break his arm, but a sling was put on as a precaution, the Vatican spokesman said in a statement On Dec.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kirby 'confident' Americans in Gaza will be freed Sunday amid reports of Israel, Hamas deal holdup
item.description: The White House national security communications adviser John Kirby said he is "confident" a deal will be implemented on Sunday despite Israeli claims that Hamas has backtracked on deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu says 'last-minute crisis' with Hamas holding up approval of Gaza truce and hostage deal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office said Thursday his Cabinet won’t meet to approve the agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages until Hamas backs down, accusing the group of reneging on parts of the agreement in an atte
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu delays Gaza cease-fire vote, accusing Hamas of trying to back out of deal
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday accused Hamas of backing out of a cease-fire deal to release hostages and bring a pause to more than a year of fighting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mozambique's Chapo sworn in as president after disputed election
item.description: Following months of protests against his disputed election victory, Daniel Chapo of Mozambique's Frelimo party has been sworn in as president at a sparsely attended ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Puerto Rico asks Trump for help after Venezuela's Maduro threatens invasion
item.description: Puerto Rican voters have overwhelmingly chosen to keep their U.S. citizenship, granted in 1917 by an act of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Ceasefire in Gaza begins, Hamas releases hostage names to Israel
item.description: The ceasefire was delayed but will take effect Sunday after Hamas released the list of hostage names to Israeli officials.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: Biden takes victory lap in final foreign policy speech
item.description: Biden is expected to discuss wars in Ukraine, Middle East and aggression from China, Iran and North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Canada deploying 60 firefighters to help with California blazes
item.description: In a post on X, Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Canada was helping aid the Los Angeles area as several wildfires continue devastating the area.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation
